seoTitle: How to Track Time in Trello
metaDescription: Add time tracking to Trello using Power-Ups like Planyway or Everhour, log billable hours, and export reports for client invoicing.
intro: Trello doesn't include native time tracking, so recording billable hours or monitoring task velocity requires connecting a third-party Power-Up. This guide covers syncing a time tracking tool with your board and exporting reports for invoicing.

steps.heading: Open the Power-Up directory
steps.body: From your board, open the Power-Up directory to browse available time tracking integrations.

steps.heading: Add a time tracking module
steps.body: Search for and connect a tool like Planyway, Everhour, or Clockify to your board.

steps.heading: Sync the tool with your cards
steps.body: Complete the synchronization script to bridge the standalone time-tracking app with your Trello cards.

steps.heading: Choose your logging method
steps.body: Use live start-stop timers for real-time tracking, or manually log retrospective time blocks after the fact.

steps.heading: Set time estimates
steps.body: Add time estimation benchmarks to your cards so scope creep triggers an automated alert.

steps.heading: Export your reports
steps.body: Export your logged time as Excel, CSV, or HTML to handle client invoicing.

faq.question: Does Trello track time natively?
faq.answer: No, Trello doesn't include built-in time tracking, so you'll need a Power-Up like Planyway, Everhour, or Clockify.

faq.question: Can I log time after the fact instead of using a live timer?
faq.answer: Yes, most time tracking Power-Ups support both live start-stop timers and manual retrospective entries.

faq.question: Can I export time data for client invoices?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically export your logged time as Excel, CSV, or HTML reports for invoicing.

faq.question: Will I be alerted if a task goes over budget?
faq.answer: Yes, setting time estimation benchmarks lets the tool flag scope creep with automated alerts.
